title: Alismorkis uncata
taxonomy: Alismorkis uncata is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Alismorkis, which is a small group of orchids native to tropical Africa. The family Orchidaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The genus Alismorkis is further divided into several species, with Alismorkis uncata being one of them. Alismorkis uncata is often categorized as a terrestrial orchid due to its growth habits and habitat preferences.

Its extracts have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. intro: Alismorkis uncata, also known as the Uncata orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
